title: Context Rot：越长越不准
title_en: Context Rot: Longer Isn't Always Better

body.problem: 开发者以为 context window 越大越好，于是把所有信息一股脑塞进去。结果发现随着 token 数量增长，模型的准确率和信息检索能力反而下降——这种现象叫做 context rot。
body.solution: 主动管理 context window 中的内容质量，而不只是关注容量。使用 server-side compaction（服务端压缩）来自动精简长对话；使用 context editing（上下文编辑）来手动清理不需要的 tool result 和 thinking block。核心原则：精选放进 context 的内容，和拥有多大空间一样重要。
body.keyTakeaway: Context window 管理的关键不是「能塞多少」，而是「该放什么」。

body_en.problem: Developers assume bigger context windows are always better and stuff everything in. But as token count grows, accuracy and recall degrade—a phenomenon called context rot.
body_en.solution: Actively curate what's in the context window, not just how much fits. Use server-side compaction to automatically trim long conversations; use context editing to manually clear unnecessary tool results and thinking blocks. Core principle: what you put in context matters as much as how much space you have.
body_en.keyTakeaway: Context window management is about 'what to include', not just 'how much fits'.
